title: Continued Rapid Ohia Death Response Act of 2025
short_title: Continued Rapid Ohia Death Response Act of 2025
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
plain_summary: This bill helps Hawaii fight a deadly tree fungus called Rapid Ohia Death that has already killed over one million native trees. The government will require several agencies to work together with Hawaii to study the disease, control wild animals that spread it, and help restore the damaged forests. The Forest Service will give money to help stop the fungus from spreading and will support research at a special Hawaiian forestry institute. This program will continue for many years to protect Hawaii's native forests from this dangerous disease.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Continued Rapid Ohia Death Response Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has learned that a fungus called Ceratocystis, which people call Rapid Ohia Death, has killed more than one million native trees in Hawaii. Congress also knows that the Secretary of Agriculture has been giving money and workers to help with this problem. The Agriculture Department has been working to find and identify Rapid Ohia Death, stop it from spreading to more trees, find Ohia trees that can fight off the disease, and grow more of these strong trees to replant Hawaii's native forests.

sections.title: Section 3 Definitions
sections.text: In this law, Rapid Ohia Death means the fungus described earlier that has killed more than one million native trees in Hawaii. When this law says State, it means the State of Hawaii.

sections.title: Section 4 Collaboration
sections.text: The Secretary of the Interior must work together with the Secretary of Agriculture and the State of Hawaii to fight Rapid Ohia Death.

sections.title: Section 5 Sustained Efforts
sections.text: The Secretary of the Interior must keep doing research on how Rapid Ohia Death spreads from place to place. This work will be done through the United States Geological Survey. The Secretary of the Interior must also keep working with Hawaii and local groups to control ungulates (hoofed animals like pigs and deer) in areas where they are fighting Rapid Ohia Death. This work will be done through the United States Fish and Wildlife Service and will happen on federal land, state land, and private land. The Secretary of Agriculture must keep providing money to help stop Rapid Ohia Death from spreading and to restore Hawaii's native forests. Some of this money will go to the Secretary of the Interior. The Agriculture Secretary must also keep providing workers and money for buildings and equipment to the Institute of Pacific Islands Forestry so they can study Rapid Ohia Death. This work will be done through the Forest Service.

sections.title: Section 6 Authorization of Appropriations
sections.text: Congress allows five million dollars to be spent each year from twenty twenty six through twenty thirty six to carry out this law. This money can be used for work done by the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of Agriculture, or both of them together.
